predicted source of confusion: A cue type such as prophecy, testimony, or divination may be mistaken for the visible act performed with it.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: Ask whether the problem is what this cue type does here, or what an act of interpretation, consultation, confession, recognition, or self-reading does.
expected direction of error: Reserved cue_function may be overused when operation_function is the more specific locus.
whether the pair has prior positive exam: operation_function has prior positive examples; cue_function has no positive demo example

predicted source of confusion: A cue family may be confused with the source or medium receiving warranting force.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: Ask whether the cue type itself is underdetermined after excluding more specific loci, or whether one source, sign, medium, speaker, or result is granted standing.
expected direction of error: Reserved cue_function may be overused when warrant_attribution is the more specific locus.
whether the pair has prior positive exam: warrant_attribution has prior positive examples; cue_function has no positive demo example

predicted source of confusion: A single source granted authority may be confused with an interaction among multiple warrants.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: Count the warrants: if the annotation depends on how more than one warrant interacts, use warrant_relation; if one source receives authority, use warrant_attribution.
expected direction of error: One-warrant cases may be overcoded as warrant_relation when contextual complexity is present.

predicted source of confusion: Authority is often assigned through an interpretive act, so granting standing may be confused with visible operation.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: If evidence becomes usable as a warrant through the operation, prefer warrant_attribution; if evidence already has standing and the function depends on handling it, prefer operation_function.
expected direction of error: Warrant attribution may be overcoded as operation_function when interpretation is visibly present.

predicted source of confusion: Cross-warrant conflict may coexist with retrospective framing or later readability.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: If removing cross-warrant conflict removes the function, prefer warrant_relation; if removing the later temporal frame removes it, prefer temporal_layering.
expected direction of error: Warrant conflicts in retrospectively framed cases may be mistaken for temporal_layering, or temporal effects may be flattened into warrant_relation.

predicted source of confusion: Incompatible testimony or standpoint can look like conflict among warrants or like perspective control.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: Ask whether incompatibility among multiple warrants determines the function, or whether changing the speaker/testimonial position would change the annotation.
expected direction of error: Perspective-governed testimony may be overcoded as warrant_relation when multiple accounts are visible.

predicted source of confusion: An operation can matter because it performs conversion or because it creates, exposes, ranks, or intensifies relations among warrants.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: If the operation itself performs the conversion, prefer operation_function; if it mainly relates multiple warrants, prefer warrant_relation.
expected direction of error: Operations that rank warrants may be overcoded as operation_function when warrant_relation is dominant.

predicted source of confusion: Recognition or fulfilment can be treated as a local operation or as later reclassification of earlier evidence.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: If the local operation produces the function, prefer operation_function; if a later moment reclassifies earlier evidence, prefer temporal_layering.
expected direction of error: Later reclassification may be overcoded as operation_function when a recognition scene is visible.

predicted source of confusion: A scope or unit boundary may be confused with the relation among warrants inside a stable unit.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: Ask whether changing the boundary of the annotation would change the result; if the boundary is stable but warrants interact, use warrant_relation.
expected direction of error: Boundary_setting may be underused when coders move directly to warrant_relation inside an unstable unit.
whether the pair has prior positive exam: warrant_relation has prior positive examples; boundary_setting has no positive demo example

predicted source of confusion: A missing boundary may be confused with a missing contextual bridge.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: Ask whether the relevant unit/scope is unstable, or whether the unit is stable but needs external contextual inference to support conversion.
expected direction of error: Boundary problems may be absorbed into context_inference as a residual explanation.
whether the pair has prior positive exam: neither boundary_setting nor context_inference has a positive demo example in the cited manual lineage

predicted source of confusion: Later fulfilment, retrospective readability, or reuse may coexist with local warrant conflict.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: If removing the later temporal frame removes the function, prefer temporal_layering; if removing cross-warrant conflict removes it, prefer warrant_relation.
expected direction of error: Temporal_layering may be undercoded when later readability appears alongside multiple warrants.

predicted source of confusion: A visible recognition or fulfilment scene can look like a local operation.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: If the function is produced by local operation, prefer operation_function; if a later moment reclassifies earlier evidence, prefer temporal_layering.
expected direction of error: Temporal effects may be overcoded as operation_function when the text includes a visible recognition act.

predicted source of confusion: Perspective, witness role, or testimonial position may be confused with incompatibility among multiple accounts as warrants.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: Ask whether changing the speaker/testimonial position would change the annotation; if incompatibility among multiple warrants is dominant, use warrant_relation.
expected direction of error: Perspective_assignment may be undercoded when multiple accounts make warrant conflict salient.

predicted source of confusion: Confession or testimony can be treated as a visible operation or as standpoint-governed perspective.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: Ask whether the function depends on the act of confessing/testifying or on whose perspective/testimonial role controls the annotation.
expected direction of error: Perspective-governed testimony may be overcoded as operation_function when the speech act is prominent.

predicted source of confusion: Historical readability or later framing may be mistaken for external contextual inference.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: Ask whether later readability is the decisive feature; if yes, use temporal_layering rather than context_inference.
expected direction of error: Context_inference may be overused when temporal_layering provides the more specific explanation.
whether the pair has prior positive exam: temporal_layering has prior positive examples; context_inference has no positive demo example

predicted source of confusion: External or background context may be confused with interaction among warrants within the text.
counterfactual test intended to distingu: Ask whether the local text contains interacting warrants; if yes, use warrant_relation. Use context_inference only when the missing contextual bridge is dominant.
expected direction of error: Context_inference may be overused as a residual category for difficult warrant interactions.
whether the pair has prior positive exam: warrant_relation has prior positive examples; context_inference has no positive demo example
